sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) properly, the open hand, the palm, in all its occurrences used as the measure of four fingers [a hand-breadth], 1Ki 7:26 2Ch 4:5 comp. Jer 52:21. Psa 39:6, הִנֵּה טְפָחֹות נָחַתָּה יָמַי “behold, thou hast made my days as handbreadths,” i.e. very short.

(2) in architecture, mutuli; i.e. projecting stones, on the tops of which beams rest (Kragfteine), 1Ki 7:9. LXX. τὰ γείσεα.
